subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: +2.5% | TSM past TP1 — trim recommended
html_body:

⚠️ nc = day change unconfirmed (prior close not retrieved). AVGO ex-div $0.65 on 22 Jun (you earned $0.20 on 0.315 shares).
🚨 Exit Alerts & Level Check
🔔 TSM — TP1 HIT. Execute trim now.
Stop: $430 (just below prior TP1 level now acting as support; trailing up from stored $357 — that level has no structure behind it anymore) | TP2: $485 (Barclays/Needham cluster $470–480, next resistance) | TP3: $575 (Susquehanna bull target)
Sell 0.112 TSM at market (~$462.12)
Gross proceeds: $51.76 | Exit commission: −$3.00 | Net: $48.76
Cost basis of sold shares: $43.49
Gross P&L: +$8.27 | NET P&L: +$5.27 ✅
TSM set a new 52-week high intraday ($473.50) on Jun 22 before closing at $462.12. The TP1 at $435 was crossed weeks ago per portfolio notes. Exit strategy says sell 50% here. Remaining 0.112 shares ride to TP2 $485 with stop now at $430 (protect the gain). Also: update stop to $430 in portfolio.json.
After trim: 0.112 TSM remaining, cost basis $43.50, new stop $430. If TSM hits $430 stop: exit 0.112 at $430 → gross $48.16, net $45.16 vs cost $43.50 → still net +$1.66. Protected.
🔔 LLY — AT TP1. Commission blocks trim. HOLD.
Stop: $1,050 (near-term floor; stored $915 is catastrophic-level, real support cluster $1,050–1,060 from short-term MA base) | TP1: $1,114 (tested — only $2.78 away at close) | TP2: $1,243 | TP3: $1,393
If trimming 30% (0.02958 shares) at TP1 $1,114:
Gross proceeds: $32.97 | Exit commission: −$3.00 | Net: $29.97
Cost basis of sold shares: $29.43
Gross P&L: +$3.54 | NET P&L: +$0.54 — commission eats 85% of profit
LLY closed at $1,111.22, within $2.78 of TP1 at $1,114. The position is only 0.0986 shares ($109.57 total). A 30% trim frees only $33 in proceeds — $3 commission = 9.1% drag. Gross P&L barely clears the $3 commission floor. HOLD. Let this run to TP2 $1,243 where the position size relative to commission cost becomes meaningful. Orforglipron (Foundayo) launch is a live catalyst. Sell only on a decisive break above $1,250+ or thesis change.
⚠️ NVDA — 2.2% FROM STOP. Watch closely.
Stop: $204 (just below $205 short-term MA + pre-May breakout base — structure confirmed) | TP1: $240 (15% above, prior resistance + analyst cluster) | TP2: $260 | TP3: $285 (BofA range)
NVDA closed $208.65, down −0.97% on Jun 22. Distance to stop: ($208.65−$204) / $208.65 = 2.2% — inside the alert window. June 22 range was $208.62–$213.99, suggesting selling pressure capped at $214. This is not a volume surge; low-volume drifts through a level don't trigger the stop — high-volume breaks do. WATCH for follow-through selling. If NVDA opens below $208 on volume, the $204 stop becomes live. Next catalyst: Q2 FY27 earnings August 19, 2026 (57 days out). Hyperscaler capex commentary remains supportive. Hold unless stop is hit.
✅ AVGO — HOLD. Recovery intact.
Stop: $379 (just below post-earnings low $386.62 on Jun 5 — solid market structure, confirmed) | TP1: $474 (prior resistance) | TP2: $516 (analyst consensus avg)
AVGO closed $411.35 — essentially flat vs entry $412.04 (−$0.22). Stock went ex-div $0.65 on Jun 22, adding $0.20 to your account. Recovery from the post-earnings flush ($386.62 low Jun 5) looks complete. AI Q3 guidance $16B (3× YoY) remains the thesis. Analyst consensus $516.91, range $390–$650. Stop at $379 is tight and structural. Next catalysts: August hyperscaler capex reports (MSFT/META read-through). Hold.
⏳ Pending Watchlist — Waiting on IBKR Approval
VRDN WATCH   6/10
Viridian Therapeutics — Thyroid Eye Disease
🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock
Current price: $16.63  |  Entry target: $20.00   ⚠️ Price dropped 16.9% below target — ENTRY MOVED, levels need re-check
Stop: $14.50 (now only 12.8% below price, tight)  |  TP1: $28.00  |  TP2: $37.00
🔴 URGENT: 7 DAYS TO PDUFA  — Veligrotug FDA decision June 30, 2026
Approval probability:
82% — BLA with Priority Review + Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Field team commercially ready. Phase 3 data: consistent proptosis response and diplopia resolution.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (interest income vs milestone revenue ratio unclear)
🚨 ONLY 7 DAYS TO PDUFA — if IBKR not approved by Jun 27, the entry window before the binary event closes. At current price $16.63 (vs target $20.00), the position risk/reward has shifted — original stop $14.50 is now only 12.8% below vs the original 27.5% downside buffer. Re-run "analyze VRDN" to update entry/stop before any position.
VERA BUY NOW   7/10
Vera Therapeutics — IgA Nephropathy
🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock
Current price: $35.09  |  Entry target: $34.04   ✅ Entry still valid — price +3.1% above target, within range
Stop: $24.50 (director Enright open-market buy zone)  |  TP1: $55.00  |  TP2: $78.00
🟡 SOON: 14 DAYS TO PDUFA  — Atacicept FDA decision July 7, 2026
Approval probability:
89% — Priority Review BLA. FDA aligned on earlier eGFR analysis (Q3 2026 vs 2027) — accelerated full approval pathway. ORIGIN 3 interim: proteinuria −46% vs placebo, published NEJM. IgAN analog base rate ~80%; FDA alignment on eGFR timeline is a conviction lift.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — pre-revenue, term debt structure needs Musaffa check
When IBKR approved: enter $240 target position at ~$35 (approx 6.85 shares), stop $24.50. PDUFA July 7 gives 14-day window. Entry is essentially at target. Market cap ~$3.2B — above the $2B small-cap filter but thesis is pure binary catalyst.
📡 Catalyst Plays — 23 Jun 2026
Radar searched 15 sources across FDA, clinical readouts, options signals. Note: strongest near-term binary plays (VRDN/VERA) are already in your watchlist above. Below are the next best candidates.
CAPR⏰ WATCH — 60 DAYS OUT
Capricor Therapeutics — Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy Cardiomyopathy
Catalyst: PDUFA August 22, 2026 — Deramiocel BLA (60 days out — fails 45-day gate, showing for tracking)
Market cap: ~$1.55B  |  Cash: $318M (runway through 2027)  |  Short interest: 20.1% float, 12.7 days to cover
Probability: ~65% — Phase 3 HOPE-3 met primary + key secondary endpoints (54% slowing of upper limb progression, 91% cardiac ejection fraction benefit). FDA lifted prior CRL, resumed review, no new review issues identified. DMD rare disease base rate ~60%; derisked by HOPE-3 success.
Scenarios:
✅ Approval: +40–60% (orphan drug premium). 💀 CRL: −40–50% (prior CRL history). EV on 30% position (~$230): +$69 / −$69.
Smart money: 20.1% short interest creates squeeze fuel on approval.
Cash runway: $318M, zero dilution risk near-term.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — pre-revenue biotech, check Musaffa
NOT actionable yet — 60 days exceeds the 45-day catalyst entry window. Add to watch on July 8. High short interest means the approval pop could be amplified. Say "analyze CAPR" closer to August for full deep dive.
ASML⬇️ FALLBACK LARGE-CAP
ASML Holding — EUV Lithography Monopoly (Tier A fallback, no near-term binary catalyst)
No PDUFA-style binary event. Secular AI capex build-out = sustained demand. Relevant as a rotation target if trimming semi concentration. Halal-compliant (Musaffa verified). Earnings expected Q3 2026.
Conviction: 3/5 as new entry today (no near-term catalyst to drive alpha). Better as replacement if a semi position is exited.
NVO⬇️ FALLBACK LARGE-CAP
Novo Nordisk — GLP-1 Duopoly (Tier A fallback, LLY peer)
LLY peer in the GLP-1 trade. Diversifies within the same thesis. Relevant if LLY concentration grows. Halal-compliant status — check Musaffa for current quarter. No near-term binary catalyst.
Today's radar found no new small-cap catalyst plays outside the watchlist within 45 days. CAPR at 60 days is the closest candidate. VRDN and VERA dominate the near-term binary calendar for your halal-eligible universe.
💵 Cash Deployment
Current cash: $213.81  |  Less $6 reserve: $207.81 deployable
After TSM trim (if executed): cash rises to ~$262.57 → $256.57 deployable.
30% portfolio cap on single catalyst position: 0.30 × $844.39 = $253.32 max
Option A — Watchlist priority: Cash is best held as dry powder for VERA (target $240 position if IBKR approves, PDUFA July 7) or VRDN (PDUFA June 30 — 7 days, decision imminent). Both fit within the 30% position cap. Recommended: do nothing until IBKR responds.
Option B — Add to LLY: LLY risk score is LOW-MODERATE (39/100), thesis intact. But at $1,111 with $207 to deploy, you'd get 0.186 shares. Commission $3 = 1.4% of transaction — acceptable. Blended entry rises to ~$1,003. But this increases GLP-1 concentration and LLY is approaching TP1. Not the right entry point.
Recommended: Hold cash. VRDN pdufa is June 30 — 7 days. VERA pdufa is July 7 — 14 days. The cash is spoken for. If IBKR approves in the next 5 days, deploy into VERA (~$240, entry valid at $35.09). If both windows pass unexecuted, re-evaluate fresh in July.
🔄 Rotation & Portfolio Adjustments
🔄 Suggested action: Execute TSM TP1 trim + update stop
  • Sell: 0.112 TSM at market (~$462.12). Net proceeds ~$48.76 after $3 commission.
  • Why: TSM set a new 52-wk high ($473.50) intraday Jun 22, then closed $462.12 — classic distribution signal. You're 19% up on cost. Exit strategy says 50% at TP1. Remaining 0.112 shares ride to TP2 $485 and beyond.
  • Update stop on remaining 0.112 TSM: Trail from $357 → $430 (just below TP1, locks in minimum gain on remainder).
  • Risk: TSM could rip to $485+ without pause if AI foundry demand beats; you'd leave money on the table. Acceptable — half your position still captures that upside.
  • Deploy proceeds: Hold as dry powder for VERA/VRDN IBKR approval.
Your call. Confirm before executing.
⚠️ Risk scorecard
TICKERSCORELABELKEY DRIVER
NVDA35/100MODERATEPremium valuation (40× fwd P/E), offset by 80% ROIC + 50%+ growth. Main risk: stop proximity.
LLY39/100MODERATEGLP-1 duopoly moat. Orforglipron launch live. Risk: NVO competition intensifying.
AVGO44/100MODERATEPost-earnings reset, recovering. AI networking thesis intact but full-year guidance disappointment risk lingers.
TSM41/100MODERATECheap valuation (25× P/E). Key tail risk: Taiwan Strait geopolitical event = binary −50% scenario regardless of fundamentals.
No position scores HIGH or EXTREME. Semi concentration (NVDA+AVGO+TSM = 82% of positions) is the structural risk, not individual fundamentals.
📚 Today's Concept — #25 of 30
Block Trades & Dark Pools
Most retail trades happen on NYSE or Nasdaq — visible to everyone. But institutions (hedge funds, mutual funds, pension desks) move thousands of shares through dark pools: private trading venues where orders don't appear on the public order book. When a large block clears, it prints to the consolidated tape, often after the move already happened in the lit market. This is why you saw TSM spike to $473.50 in premarket Jun 22 then close at $462.12 — the premarket move was likely institutional positioning; the fade was the real-size print clearing. When a stock gaps 1-2% on zero news mid-session, you're usually watching the reaction to a block that cleared in a dark pool seconds earlier. Cheddar Flow and Unusual Whales track these prints in near real-time. You can't front-run them, but recognizing the pattern stops you from chasing what looks like news-driven action.
Also today: Sweep orders — when an institution buys/sells across multiple exchanges simultaneously to fill large size fast. Shows up on options scanners as cross-exchange unusual flow; often a directional signal before the block prints.
Prices as of 22 Jun 2026 close (reviewing in Kuwait 6 AM, 23 Jun 2026).
Sources: Yahoo Finance (NVDA $208.65, LLY $1,111.22, AVGO $411.35); CNBC (TSM $462.12); MarketBeat (VRDN $16.63 ≈Jun 19); CNBC (VERA $35.09); Investopedia, BiopharmaWatch, CatalystAlert (FDA calendar).
AVGO/TSM day % change unconfirmed — prior close not retrieved.

This is personal research, not financial advice. All buy/sell decisions are yours. Halal verification should be confirmed on Musaffa before entering any new position. Commission math uses $3/trade. Always confirm stop/TP levels with live charts before executing.
